My rapid rating on Lichess I think is 2200, but it's only because I played like five games and my daughter was sitting next to me, kind of playing them with me, so we were telling each other moves and I won them all and I stopped. I will tell you that my chess.com rating is like 850, and people are shocked it's that low, but there are so many games where I'm putting the kids to bed, sneaking and playing, then I have to quit. But Lichess is more my real rating because I take it more seriously. I think my real Lichess rating is probably like 1800 or 1700. My daughter Georgia is obsessed with chess. She works with a chess tutor, plays like three hours a day, especially in summer, five hours a day. She goes to the park. She's a good player. She's beating people who are like 2200s.
turns.zh: 哇。我跟你说，这甚至不是谦虚的炫耀，是虚假的炫耀。我在 Lichess 上的快棋等级分我觉得是 2200，但那只是因为我下了大概五盘棋，我女儿坐在旁边，跟我一起下，我们互相告诉对方走法，我全赢了，然后就不下了。我告诉你，我在 chess.com 上的等级分大概是 850，人们都很震惊这么低，但有很多次我在哄孩子睡觉时偷偷下，然后不得不退出。但 Lichess 更接近我的真实水平，因为我更认真。我觉得我真实的 Lichess 等级分大概是 1800 或 1700。我女儿 Georgia 痴迷于国际象棋。她跟教练学，每天下大约三小时，尤其是夏天，每天五小时。她去公园下。她是个好棋手。她能打败 2200 左右的人。

turns.en: What's her USCF rating?
turns.zh: 她的 USCF 等级分是多少？

turns.en: I don't know. She's only been in one tournament in London and did shockingly well. She lost a game where a guy convinced her to draw in a game she would have won. She's only 15. She needs to get a little stronger emotionally, but she wants to spend the summer doing tournaments. She really wants to become a master or grandmaster.
turns.zh: 我不知道。她只参加过伦敦的一次比赛，表现惊人地好。她输了一盘棋，对方说服她和棋，而她本来能赢。她才 15 岁。她需要在情绪上更强一些，但她想整个夏天都参加比赛。她真的很想成为大师或特级大师。

turns.en: I'm in the US a master.
turns.zh: 我在美国是大师。

turns.en: Yeah, she's beating people with 2200s. So let's connect you, me, and her. You'll have better games on Lichess with her than me.
turns.zh: 是啊，她能打败 2200 的人。所以我们三个连一下。你在 Lichess 上跟她下会比跟我下更好。

turns.en: Yeah. No, it's fun. I mean, I take lessons every week. I'm trying to get back to my peak USCF of about 2250, at an older age, and it's impossible.
turns.zh: 是啊。不，这很有趣。我每周都上课。我试图回到我 USCF 的巅峰大约 2250，但年纪大了，不可能了。

turns.en: Oh, really? Yeah. I'm definitely not learning and picking up as fast as her, but I can still beat her. It's more because I'm her dad and I know her emotional weaknesses and I can exploit those.
turns.zh: 哦，真的吗？是啊。我肯定不如她学得快、掌握得快，但我还能赢她。这更多是因为我是她爸爸，知道她的情绪弱点，可以利用这些。

turns.en: That's brutal.
turns.zh: 真狠。

turns.en: But I never let kids win. So you can't either.
turns.zh: 但我从不让孩子赢。所以你也不能。

ts.sec: Book Writing: Balancing Stories and Lessons

turns.en: Mark, author of Life at the Speed of Play. I thoroughly enjoyed the book. I had this pit in my stomach the whole time writing, like I just don't want it to be boring. There are so many business books that I find painful and I get annoyed at the author because they're making obvious points, doing this playbook. I am ignoring some of the proven ideas at my own risk, but I just didn't want boring dry content. I learned by the end that whatever I had fun talking about and writing about, I thought would be fun to read. There is a trade-off in the book because some chapters, like going deep on proven better new, are more dry and professorial but more valuable. There is always this trade-off between stories being engaging and fun and you kind of learn. My co-author co-authored Ben Horowitz's book, The Hard Thing About Hard Things. They made half the book stories and half lessons. In some ways, that's smarter, and I was more ambitious. I just didn't want it to be boring. I feel like you did that though. You told a lot of stories in this book and it's fascinating, and it's kind of historical because you really go back to the 90s and 2000s where all this first blossomed. I hate to be like the OG, but there are so many lessons to learn from each era. When you pull back the camera and look at what people have done before, you'll be more successful and less caught up in little races. You don't have to build a coding agent just because that's massively working right now.
turns.zh: Mark，《Life at the Speed of Play》的作者。我非常喜欢这本书。我写作时一直有种不安，就是不想让它无聊。有太多商业书让我觉得痛苦，我对作者感到恼火，因为他们讲显而易见的东西，搞这种套路。我冒险忽略了一些已被证明的想法，但就是不想写枯燥的内容。最后我明白了，只要我聊得开心、写得开心，读起来也会有趣。书中有个权衡，有些章节，比如深入探讨已被证明更好的新事物，更枯燥、更学术，但也更有价值。故事更吸引人、更有趣，你也能学到东西，这两者之间总有取舍。我的合著者曾合著了 Ben Horowitz 的《The Hard Thing About Hard Things》。他们让书一半是故事，一半是教训。从某些方面看，那更聪明，而我更雄心勃勃。我只是不想让它无聊。我觉得你做到了。你在书里讲了很多故事，很吸引人，而且有点历史感，因为你真的回溯到 90 年代和 2000 年代，那时这一切刚刚兴起。我不想自称 OG，但每个时代都有很多教训可以学习。当你退后一步，看看别人以前做过什么，你会更成功，不会陷入这些小竞赛。你不必仅仅因为编码智能体现在很火就去构建一个。

turns.en: Or maybe you should, you know, but also there's so many other topics. We can do this again because I think you're in Chicago.
turns.zh: 或者也许你应该，你知道，但还有很多其他话题。我们可以再做一次，因为我想你在芝加哥。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: That's cool. I grew up in Chicago, so I have a lot of Chicago.
turns.zh: 那很酷。我在芝加哥长大，所以我对芝加哥很熟悉。

turns.en: So, oh yeah, that's right.
turns.zh: 所以，哦，是的，没错。

turns.en: How often are you here?
turns.zh: 你多久来一次这里？

turns.en: I'm going to be there in the middle of July because I still have family there and we have a wedding.
turns.zh: 我七月中旬会去那里，因为我还有家人在那里，而且我们要参加一个婚礼。

turns.en: Let's do a podcast when you're in town.
turns.zh: 等你来城里的时候，我们做一期播客吧。

turns.en: Oh, yeah. We could do it in person. That'd be fun.
turns.zh: 哦，是的。我们可以当面做。那会很有趣。

turns.en: Yeah. And bring the chessboard. I'll bring the chessboard.
turns.zh: 是的。带上棋盘。我会带棋盘。

turns.en: Okay. And we'll bring Georgia so at least she can give you a serious game.
turns.zh: 好的。我们会带上乔治娅，至少她能跟你认真下一盘。

turns.en: Excellent. Excellent. Well, Mark, thanks once again. Life at the speed of play. Great book and I look forward to continuing the conversation.
turns.zh: 太好了。太好了。那么，马克，再次感谢。《游戏速度的生活》是本好书，我期待继续我们的对话。

turns.en: Yeah. Fun.
turns.zh: 是的。很有趣。

consensus.en: All new fails; statistically, most new products fail, so start with proven concepts.
consensus.zh: 所有新事物都会失败；统计上大多数新产品都会失败，因此应从已被验证的概念开始。

consensus.en: Use the 'proven better new' framework: copy proven products, make them better, add small innovation.
consensus.zh: 使用“已验证·更好·创新”框架：复制已被验证的产品，使其更好，再添加微小创新。

consensus.en: Consumer apps are currently uninvestable due to distribution challenges and lack of viral platforms.
consensus.zh: 由于分发挑战和缺乏病毒式平台，消费者应用目前不可投资。

consensus.en: AI coding tools enable rapid prototyping, but testing and failing fast is not yet widely practiced.
consensus.zh: AI 编码工具支持快速原型开发，但快速测试和失败的做法尚未广泛普及。

consensus.en: Enterprise and prosumer markets offer better opportunities than consumer apps right now.
consensus.zh: 企业级和产消者市场目前比消费者应用提供更好的机会。

contrarian.en: Perfectly copying a successful app without being accused is the magic trick.
contrarian.zh: 完美复制成功的应用而不被指责是魔术般的技巧。

contrarian.en: Gaming industry has regressed in testing speed due to compiled code on mobile.
contrarian.zh: 由于移动端编译代码，游戏行业在测试速度上已经倒退。

contrarian.en: AI agents can network autonomously, creating a new social membrane for lead generation.
contrarian.zh: AI 智能体可以自主联网，创建新的社交膜层以生成线索。

contrarian.en: Midjourney's terrible Discord UI is part of its proven success; copying it would be wise.
contrarian.zh: Midjourney 糟糕的 Discord 界面是其成功的一部分；复制它是明智的。

contrarian.en: Instagram and Facebook have negative NPS after users quit, like quitting cigarettes.
contrarian.zh: Instagram 和 Facebook 在用户退出后净推荐值为负，如同戒烟。
